metadata.name: settlement
metadata.description: Stoppage settlement: verify TxLINE proofs via CPI, mark markets settled

instructions.name: resolve_market
docs: Resolve a market by verifying a TxLINE Merkle proof on-chain.
1. CPI into TxLINE's `validate_stat` with the full proof data
2. Read the return data (bool) — true means the predicate holds
3. Verify the result matches the expected outcome
4. Emit `MarketResolved` carrying the full proof data
Permissionless: any keeper can call this. The proof itself is the
authority — if `validate_stat` rejects it, the call fails and the
entire transaction reverts (including any force_settle in the same tx).
The `txline_ix_data` is the pre-built instruction data for TxLINE's
`validate_stat` instruction (discriminator + borsh-serialized args).
Building it in the SDK keeps the complex TxLINE type serialization
in TypeScript, where the TxLINE types are already defined.

accounts.name: resolver
docs: Permissionless keeper — anyone can call resolve_market. The
proof is the authority, not the caller.

accounts.name: market
docs: The market being resolved. Owned by programs/market.

accounts.name: txline_program
docs: The address is verified by the CPI call — if it's not the real
TxLINE program, validate_stat will fail.

accounts.name: daily_scores_merkle_roots
docs: The daily_scores_merkle_roots PDA from TxLINE. This is the account
that stores the anchored Merkle root for the given epoch day.
The PDA is derived as: ["daily_scores_roots", epoch_day_u16_le]

errors.name: InvalidReturnSource
errors.msg: Return data did not come from the TxLINE program

errors.name: ProofOutcomeMismatch
errors.msg: Proof outcome does not match validate_stat result

types.name: MarketResolved
docs: The proof-carrying resolution event. This is what the Verifiable
Resolution UI renders: the raw statement, the anchored root, the
outcome, and a hash of the Merkle path. A judge or user can
independently verify the proof against the anchored root without
trusting Stoppage.

fields.name: market
docs: The market that was resolved.

fields.name: statement
docs: The raw statement being proven, e.g. "GOAL:FRA:63:00".

fields.name: merkle_root
docs: The Merkle root anchored on Solana (from TxLINE's daily_scores_roots PDA).

fields.name: outcome
docs: The outcome: 0=YES, 1=NO, 2=VOID.

fields.name: proof_hash
docs: Keccak-256 hash of the Merkle root — compact on-chain reference.

fields.name: resolver
docs: Who called resolve_market (permissionless — anyone).

fields.name: timestamp
docs: On-chain timestamp of resolution.

fields.name: validated_on_chain
docs: Whether the proof was verified on-chain via validate_stat CPI.
